gold_answer: Culpable homicide is murder if the act causing death is done: (a) with the intention of causing death; (b) with the intention of causing bodily injury that the offender knows is likely to cause the death of the person to whom the harm is caused; (c) with the intention of causing bodily injury that is sufficient in the ordinary course of nature to cause death; or (d) with the knowledge that the act is so imminently dangerous that it must in all probability cause death, without any excuse for incurring the risk (subject to the Sanhita's exceptions).
